seoTitle: How to Get an Airtable API Key: Find & Create
metaDescription: Generate an Airtable Personal Access Token from the Developer Hub, scope it with read/write permissions, and assign base access securely.
intro: Airtable replaced its legacy API key with Personal Access Tokens, which give you finer control over what an integration can actually do. This guide covers finding the Developer Hub, creating a new token with the right scopes, and assigning it to specific bases so it only has as much access as it needs.

steps.heading: Open your account menu
steps.body: Click your account profile icon from the main Airtable dashboard.

steps.heading: Go to the Developer Hub
steps.body: Select the Developer Hub option to access API configuration settings.

steps.heading: Start creating a token
steps.body: Navigate to the Personal Access Tokens tab and click 'Create token.'

steps.heading: Name your token and set scopes
steps.body: Give the token a clear name and select the required data scopes, such as read and write permissions.

steps.heading: Assign base access
steps.body: Choose the specific bases you want this token to be able to access, rather than granting account-wide access.

steps.heading: Generate and save it securely
steps.body: Click Create to generate your unique alphanumeric token, then copy and store it securely — you won't be able to view the full value again.

faq.question: Is a Personal Access Token the same thing as an API key?
faq.answer: It's the modern replacement for the legacy API key, serving the same authentication purpose but with more granular, scoped permissions.

faq.question: What scopes should I select when creating a token?
faq.answer: Choose only the read and write permissions your specific integration actually needs, rather than granting broader access than necessary.

faq.question: Can I limit a token to a single base?
faq.answer: Yes, when creating the token you specify which bases it can access, which can be one or several depending on your use case.

faq.question: What if I need to revoke a token later?
faq.answer: You can delete or regenerate tokens from the same Developer Hub, immediately cutting off any integration relying on the old value.
